# PathAp { #astroapers.PathAp }

```python
PathAp(positions, segments, holes=None, *, validate=True, plot_samples=96)
```

Aperture defined by a closed path of line segments and circular arcs.

`PathAp` supports only straight line segments and circular arcs. It does
not accept Bezier curves, spline curves, elliptical arcs, Matplotlib
``Path`` objects, or arbitrary Python predicate/callback masks. If another
curve type is flattened into line segments before construction, the
resulting aperture is exact for that flattened line-segment path, not for
the original curve.

Coordinates in `segments` and `holes` are local offsets from each aperture
center. Angles are radians counterclockwise from the positive x axis.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**positions**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple or array - like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Aperture center(s) as ``(x, y)`` pixel coordinates. Multiple centers as ``(N, 2)`` array-like.

<code>[**segments**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [sequence of tuples]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Outer contour commands. Must start with ``('move', x, y)``, followed by any mix of ``('line', x, y)`` and ``('arc', cx, cy, r, theta0, dtheta)``, and end with ``('close',)``. These are the only supported geometry commands.

<code>[**holes**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [sequence of sequences]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Zero or more hole contours, each using the same command language.

<code>[**plot_samples**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [96]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Even number >= 4 used to sample arcs for plotting.

<code>[**validate**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `False`, skip Python-side validation.
